[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In an awe-inspiring display of nature's sculptural prowess, several explorers tread carefully along the frozen shoreline. Towering beside them, an immense cliff of ice stands as a testament to the harsh, frigid climate. The sun bathes the scene in a crisp, clear light, highlighting the textures and contours of the ice, which seem to capture the essence of perpetual winter. This chilly journey, set against a vivid blue sky, offers a glimpse into the serene yet formidable beauty of polar landscapes.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2013-04-09
[TITLE]: Stunning Photos of Ice 'Waves' Captured in Antarctica | Weather.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: News Stunning Photos of Ice 'Waves' Captured in Antarctica...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Many of those who shared the photos mused that perhaps they captured a wave frozen in Antarctica's frigid temperatures just as it was about to crash onshore. But the photos show blue icebergs as they exist on the surface, which Travouillon said he stumbled upon by accident with a group of fellow scientists. 
 "It’s very rare that you see them in this position, where you can actually see the blue part of the iceberg completely uncovered with ice," he said in an interview with The Weather Channel. 
 (MORE: Incredible Photos of Australia's Peacock Spider) 
 "We walked for half an hour with a team of scientists to get to them, and then we spent a lot of time basically going around it, taking pictures at every angle we could, just touching it and enjoying its beauty."
